popularStory.headline: Dunster Castle - Historical home of the Luttrell
popularStory.description: Dunster Castle, the historical home of the Luttrell family dominates the steep hill overlooking the small picturesque village of Dunster in Somerset.  It looks out over Exmoor and the Bristol Channel …

zh.Dunster, Somerset, England: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特登斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Mohun於1308年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特登斯特時，她的父親John de Mohun IV39歲，母親Ada de Tibetot36歲。她於1334年與Sir John Luttrell在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1378年在英國戴文享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.Dunster, Somerset, England: 英国英格兰薩默塞特登斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Mohun于1308年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特登斯特时，她的父亲John de Mohun IV39岁，母亲Ada de Tibetot36岁。她于1334年与Sir John Luttrell在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1378年在英国戴文享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mohun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Mohun was born in 1308, in Dunster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Mohun IV, was 39 and her mother, Ada de Tibetot, was 36. She married Sir John Luttrell in 1334, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1378, in Devon, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Mohun est née en 1308, à Dunster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Mohun IV, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Ada de Tibetot, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Luttrell en 1334, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1378, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Mohun 1308 in Dunster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Mohun IV 39 und ihre Mutter Ada de Tibetot 36. Sie hat 1334 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Sir John Luttrell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1378 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Devon, England und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mohun è nata il nel 1308 nel Dunster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Mohun IV, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Ada de Tibetot, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Luttrell nel 1334, nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1378, nel Devon, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Somerset, England: イギリスイギリスソメルセト
ja.Dunster Castle, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトダンスター
ja.Dunster, Somerset, England: イギリスイギリスソメルセトダンスター
ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Mohunが1308年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトダンスターで生まれた時，父John de Mohun IVは39歳，母Ada de Tibetotは36歳であった。1334年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトでSir John Luttrellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1378年にイギリスデボンで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Mohun(이)가 1308년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 던스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Mohun IV(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada de Tibetot(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Sir John Luttrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1378년 영국 데폰에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mohun nasceu em 1308, em Dunster, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Mohun IV, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Ada de Tibetot, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Luttrell em 1334, em Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1378, em Devon, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Mohun родилась в 1308 г. в местности дунстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Mohun IV было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada de Tibetot, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Luttrell в 1334 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1378 г. в местности девон, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Mohun nació en 1308, en Dunster, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Mohun IV, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Ada de Tibetot, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Sir John Luttrell en 1334, en Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1378, en Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
